full_name: Service Organization Control 2 - Trust Services Criteria

rules.description: All API endpoints handling sensitive data must require authentication. No unauthenticated access to user data.
rules.section: CC6.1 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
rules.fix_guidance: Add authentication middleware (JWT, session, API key) to all sensitive endpoints. Implement rate limiting.

rules.description: All user inputs must be validated and sanitized to prevent injection attacks (SQL, XSS, command injection).
rules.section: CC6.1 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
rules.fix_guidance: Use parameterized queries. Validate input types, lengths, and formats. Sanitize HTML output. Use an ORM.

rules.rule: Error handling must not leak information
rules.description: Error messages must not expose internal system details, stack traces, database schemas, or configuration to end users.
rules.fix_guidance: Return generic error messages to users. Log detailed errors internally. Never expose stack traces in production.

rules.description: API keys, passwords, tokens, and credentials must never be hardcoded. Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
rules.section: CC6.1 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
rules.fix_guidance: Use environment variables (process.env / os.environ). Use a secrets manager. Add sensitive files to .gitignore.

rules.description: All data in transit must be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher. No HTTP endpoints in production.
rules.fix_guidance: Use HTTPS for all endpoints. Configure TLS 1.2+ minimum. Implement HSTS headers. Redirect HTTP to HTTPS.

rules.description: System must implement monitoring for security events, performance anomalies, and unauthorized access attempts.
rules.fix_guidance: Implement security event logging. Set up alerting for failed auth attempts. Monitor for unusual data access patterns.

rules.description: All code changes must go through a review process. No direct commits to production branches.
rules.fix_guidance: Enforce PR reviews. Use CI/CD pipelines with automated testing. Implement branch protection rules.
